pages.raw_text:
Delay on the part of the asphalt
trust i'll finishing the paving on mort!
Monroe street has already cost thai
| company one round sum for damages
Fred l.. Prescott, the insurance mar
and real estate operator, and hi
family, were injured last Sunday fore
noon by driving their cutter into III
unprotected light hole in the 100 block
/ ON north Monroe street The cutter
was overturned and the family tipped
out Mr.. Prescott hung onto the horse
and was dragged about SO feet. Iii,
wife was badly bruised and hurt. Then
child was also hurt.


When the contract for the paving
was awarded the asphalt people
put up a bond guaranteeing the city
against damages that might grow out
of laying the paving. AS soon as he
heard of the accident the representa-
tive of the asphalt people called on Mr.
Prescott with overtures for a settle
ment.


Mr.. Prescott said last night:


II have a check in my pocket GOV
ering all injuries received by my family
and myself. and covering the damage
done to the cutter. There was abso-
lutely no doubt but that the asphalt
company was guilty of carelessness, and
they were perfectly willing to settle
the matter"
